channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The US pulls out of peace talks with Iran
item.description: President Trump says he has cancelled the trip of his representatives to the Iran peace talks in Islamabad, shortly after Iran's Foreign Minister left Pakistan.
item.content:encoded: President Trump says he has cancelled the trip of his representatives to the Iran peace talks in Islamabad, shortly after Iran's Foreign Minister left Pakistan.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: Countries gather in Colombia for the first international conference on how to phase out fossil fuels
item.description: More than 50 countries are gathering in Santa Marta, Colombia next week for the first international conference focused on transitioning away from fossil fuels, which are the single biggest driver of climate change.
item.content:encoded: More than 50 countries are gathering in Santa Marta, Colombia next week for the first international conference focused on transitioning away from fossil fuels, which are the single biggest driver of climate change.
item.dc:creator: Julia Simon

item.title: Covering the Artemis II mission
item.description: Covering the Artemis II mission was a dream assignment for one NPR science correspondent.
item.content:encoded: Covering the Artemis II mission was a dream assignment for one NPR science correspondent.
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: "The Midnight Rebellion" is a choose-your-own-adventure podcast
item.description: WBUR's "The Midnight Rebellion" is a choose-your-own-adventure podcast. Co-creator Dean Russell tells NPR's Rob Schmitz how that works, and why it's a good way for kids to explore the climate crisis.
item.content:encoded: WBUR's "The Midnight Rebellion" is a choose-your-own-adventure podcast. Co-creator Dean Russell tells NPR's Rob Schmitz how that works, and why it's a good way for kids to explore the climate crisis.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Remembering celebrated Lebanese singer Ahmad Kaabour
item.description: A Lebanese singer famous for songs about resistance & war is laid to rest during another Israeli invasion in the region he wrote songs about.
item.content:encoded: A Lebanese singer famous for songs about resistance & war is laid to rest during another Israeli invasion in the region he wrote songs about.
item.dc:creator: Jawad Rizkallah

item.title: Former MIT president says the US is losing the innovation race to China
item.description: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with L. Rafael Reif, former president of MIT, about his recent essay in Foreign Affairs, "America Is Losing the Innovation Race: Why the Future of Science Might Be Chinese."
item.content:encoded: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with L. Rafael Reif, former president of MIT, about his recent essay in Foreign Affairs, "America Is Losing the Innovation Race: Why the Future of Science Might Be Chinese."
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: In a rare interview, a leader of the world's largest right-wing group talks to NPR
item.description: The second-in-command of the RSS, a Hindu nationalist organization in India, rarely speaks to the Western press. Here's what he said about his group's controversial history.
item.content:encoded:
The second-in-command of the RSS, a Hindu nationalist organization in India, rarely speaks to the Western press. Here's what he said about his group's controversial history.

(Image credit: IDREES MOHAMMED)
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Workers in Arizona don't have protections they need from triple-digit temperatures
item.description: Labor groups say Arizona isn't moving fast enough to establish new protections for people whose work exposes them to the state's sizzling high temperatures.
item.content:encoded: Labor groups say Arizona isn't moving fast enough to establish new protections for people whose work exposes them to the state's sizzling high temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Katherine Davis-Young

item.title: Airlines face headwinds as Iran war leads to rising fuel costs
item.description: Since the U.S. and Israel's war against Iran began, the impact on aviation has worsened. Jet fuel prices have soared. Thousands of European flights have been canceled, and one airline may stop flying.
item.content:encoded: Since the U.S. and Israel's war against Iran began, the impact on aviation has worsened. Jet fuel prices have soared. Thousands of European flights have been canceled, and one airline may stop flying.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: DoJ drops Powell probe, paving the way to confirm Kevin Warsh
item.description: The Justice Department is dropping its investigation of Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell, paving the way for the Senate to confirm Kevin Warsh, President Trump's nominee to lead the Fed.
item.content:encoded: The Justice Department is dropping its investigation of Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell, paving the way for the Senate to confirm Kevin Warsh, President Trump's nominee to lead the Fed.
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan
